name: Ginger Vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the ginger and vinegar together in a sterilized 1/2-pint mason jar.
2. Place a sterilized seal on top of the jar.
3. Set the jar aside for 1 week to steep, out of direct sunlight and away from heat.
4. Strain the contents through a fine sieve into a 2-cup glass measuring cup.
5. Clean out the sieve and rinse the Mason jar.
6. Return the strained vinegar to the jar.
7. Rinse the measuring cup.
8. Line the sieve with a moist flat-bottom coffee filter and set it over the cup.
9. Strain the vinegar through again, a bit at a time, allowing it to drip into the measuring cup.
10. Pour the vinegar into an 8-oz bottle or cruet and seal with a cork.
11. The vinegar should be ready to use immediately and will have a shelf life of at least 1 year.
